summary: Radiotherapy used in treatment of cervical cancer has been studied extensively; however, current available literature shows no established association with an elevated risk of developing colon cancer later in life. The absence of reported cases linking radiation exposure from pelvic treatments directly to colonic malignancies suggests that such a connection remains unproven clinically.
